features.name: class_with_string_member

features.name: class_with_queue_member
features.description: Code should include a SystemVerilog class declaration containing a queue-typed member variable.

features.name: class_new_constructor_declaration
features.description: Code should include a class constructor declared as new() inside a SystemVerilog class.

features.name: class_new_expression_instantiation
features.description: Code should include a class instantiation using the new expression outside the class body.

features.name: class_new_with_unsupported_string_member
features.description: Code should include a SystemVerilog class containing a string member, a new() constructor, and an external instantiation with new.

features.name: class_new_with_unsupported_queue_member
features.description: Code should include a SystemVerilog class containing a queue member, a new() constructor, and an external instantiation with new.

features.name: recursive module self-instantiation
features.description: Code should include a module that instantiates itself directly or indirectly, creating a recursive hierarchy

features.name: recursive function call
features.description: Code should include a function that calls itself without a guaranteed base-case termination

features.name: recursive generate block nesting
features.description: Code should include a generate block whose condition or body leads to unbounded self-referential expansion

metadata.snippet:
genvar i;
generate
  for (i = 0; i < N; i = i + 1) begin
    if (i < M) begin : gen_block
      // recursive generate reference
    end
  end
endgenerate

features.name: recursive parameter-dependent type definition
features.description: Code should include a parameter or typedef whose resolution depends recursively on its own definition

metadata.snippet: parameter int N = N;

features.name: bitwise_or_expression
features.description: Code should include a bitwise OR expression between two multi-bit vectors

metadata.snippet:
logic [3:0] a, b, y;
assign y = a | b;

features.name: bitwise_xor_expression
features.description: Code should include a bitwise XOR expression between two multi-bit vectors

metadata.snippet:
logic [3:0] a, b, z;
assign z = a ^ b;

features.name: majority_function_three_input
features.description: Code should include a three-input majority function that returns true when two or more inputs are true

metadata.snippet:
logic a, b, c, maj;
assign maj = (a & b) | (b & c) | (a & c);

features.name: variadic_majority_reduction
features.description: Code should include a majority reduction over an odd number of boolean inputs using sum comparison

metadata.snippet:
logic [4:0] v;
wire maj5 = $countones(v) >= 3;

features.name: aggregate_module_ports
features.description: Code should include a module with aggregate-typed ports (such as a struct or packed structure) used for both input and output directions.

features.name: instance_aggregate_port_connection
features.description: Code should include instantiation of a sub-module whose ports are aggregate types, with connections directly wired to parent module aggregate ports.

features.name: aggregate_to_scalar_lowering_with_instances
features.description: Code should include multiple modules with aggregate-typed ports where one module instantiates another, so that an aggregate-to-scalar lowering pass processes both modules and their inter-module connections.

features.name: multi-line always block with source range

features.name: multi-line if-else statement with source range
features.description: Code should include an if-else statement whose branches span multiple lines

features.name: multi-line module declaration with port list
features.description: Code should include a module declaration with a port list that spans multiple lines

features.name: multi-line always_comb block with source range

features.name: multi-line case statement with source range
features.description: Code should include a case statement with multiple case items spanning multiple lines

features.name: multi-line struct type definition with source range
features.description: Code should include a packed or unpacked struct definition that spans multiple lines

features.name: multi-line generate block with source range

metadata.snippet:
generate
  for (genvar i = 0; i < N; i++) begin : gen_loop
    assign out[i] = in[i];
  end
endgenerate

features.name: single-line assignment with column source range
features.description: Code should include a continuous or procedural assignment on a single line to exercise start-to-end column ranges

metadata.snippet: assign y = a + b;

features.name: nested ternary expression three levels deep
features.description: Code should include a ternary expression where the data operand is itself a nested ternary expression at least three levels deep

metadata.snippet:
logic [3:0] x, y, z, u, v;
logic [3:0] r = sel0 ? (sel1 ? (sel2 ? x : y) : z) : (sel3 ? u : v);

features.name: case statement with overlapping case items
features.description: Code should include a priority casez statement with overlapping bit-mask patterns that imply a choice among multiple branches

features.name: conditional assignment with constant true branch
features.description: Code should include a continuous assign with a nested ternary expression where one branch resolves to a compile-time constant

features.name: if-else chain with constant condition expression
features.description: Code should include an if-else if chain where one branch condition is a compile-time constant true expression, nested within another conditional

features.name: mux tree with fan-in from multiple select sources
features.description: Code should include a logic expression built from cascaded binary muxes where intermediate results feed into the select port of another mux

features.name: generate case with inner named scope
features.description: Code should include a generate case block containing a named scope with an inner symbol that is referenced from outside the module

metadata.snippet:

    1: begin : g_scope
      logic inner_sig;
    end
  endcase
endgenerate

features.name: hierarchical XMR into generate case scope
features.description: Code should include a cross-module reference that traverses an instance hierarchy into a generate case named scope

metadata.snippet: assign out = top.u_sub.g_case.g_scope.inner_sig;

features.name: multiple instance segments before generate scope
features.description: Code should include a hierarchical path with multiple nested instance segments preceding a generate case scope name

metadata.snippet: assign out = top.u_a.u_b.g_sel.g_blk.sig;

features.name: generate case with legalized label name
features.description: Code should include a generate case label that requires name legalization and contains a signal referenced hierarchically

metadata.snippet:
generate
  case (1'b1)
    default: begin : _1bad
      logic x;
    end
  endcase
endgenerate

features.name: generate case op alongside generate if op
features.description: Code should include both a generate case and a generate if block with named scopes to test consistent XMR path emission ordering

metadata.snippet:
generate
  if (COND) begin : g_if
    logic a;
  end
  case (MODE)
    0: begin : g_case
      logic b;
    end
  endcase
endgenerate

features.name: multiple_instances_domain_cross_dependency
features.description: Code should include multiple module instances in the same parent where a clock or domain signal from one instance flows into another instance via internal wires, creating a cross-instance dependency that affects pass ordering.

features.name: output_port_domain_annotation

features.name: domain_declaration_inst_use_before_def

features.name: signal_extract_in_cyclic_ctrl_flow
features.description: Code should include a signal created via llhd.sig in one basic block and extracted via llhd.sig.extract in another basic block that is reachable through cyclic control flow, where the Mem2Reg pass fails to properly handle SSA dominance across the cycle.

metadata.snippet:
hw.module @Foo() {
  %0 = llhd.constant_time <0ns, 0d, 1e>
  %c0_i4 = hw.constant 0 : i4
  %c0_i32 = hw.constant 0 : i32
  llhd.process {
    cf.br ^bb2(%c0_i32 : i32)
  ^bb3:
    %out = llhd.sig %c0_i4 : i4
    cf.br ^bb4(%c0_i32 : i32)
  ^bb4(%4: i32):
    cf.cond_br %false, ^bb5, ^bb6
  ^bb5:
    %c0_i2 = hw.constant 0 : i2
    %5 = llhd.sig.extract %out from %c0_i2 : (!hw.inout<i4>) -> !hw.inout<i1>
    llhd.drv %5, %false after %0 : !hw.inout<i1>
    cf.br ^bb4(%c0_i32 : i32)
  ^bb6:
    cf.br ^bb2(%c0_i32 : i32)
  }
}

features.description: Code should include an llhd.process region with multiple basic blocks forming cyclic control flow, containing llhd.sig, llhd.sig.extract, llhd.drv, and llhd.prb operations where signal definitions do not dominate all uses across backedge paths.

hw.module @Foo() {
  %0 = llhd.constant_time <0ns, 0d, 1e>
  %c0_i4 = hw.constant 0 : i4
  %false = hw.constant false
  llhd.process {
    cf.br ^bb2
  ^bb2:
    cf.cond_br %false, ^bb3, ^bb8
  ^bb3:
    %out = llhd.sig %c0_i4 : i4
    cf.br ^bb4
  ^bb4:
    cf.cond_br %false, ^bb5, ^bb6
  ^bb5:
    %5 = llhd.sig.extract %out from %c0_i2 : (!hw.inout<i4>) -> !hw.inout<i1>
    llhd.drv %5, %false after %0 : !hw.inout<i1>
    cf.br ^bb4
  ^bb8:
    llhd.wait (%c0_i4, %2 : i4), ^bb1
  }
}

hw.module @Foo() {
  %0 = llhd.constant_time <0ns, 0d, 1e>
  %c0_i2 = hw.constant 0 : i2
  %c0_i4 = hw.constant 0 : i4
  %false = hw.constant false
  llhd.process {
    cf.br ^bb4
  ^bb3:
    %out = llhd.sig %c0_i4 : i4
    cf.br ^bb4
  ^bb4:
    cf.cond_br %false, ^bb5, ^bb6
  ^bb5:

    cf.br ^bb4
  }
}

features.name: single_element_unpacked_array
features.description: Code should include an unpacked array declaration with exactly one element, using zero-based or one-based indexing

features.name: single_element_array_to_bitcast
features.description: Code should include a bitcast or conversion operation on a single-element array to a packed integer type

metadata.snippet:
wire [0:0][7:0] arr = '{8'h0};
wire [7:0] result = arr[0];

features.name: aggregate_constant_single_element
features.description: Code should include an aggregate constant with exactly one element using the '{value} syntax

features.name: priority mux tree with 2-bit selector
features.description: Code should include a priority mux tree constructed with nested ternary operators using a 2-bit selector, comparing against multiple constant values to select from different inputs

features.name: 2-bit equality comparison chain
features.description: Code should include a chain of equality comparisons using comb.icmp eq on a 2-bit selector value against multiple constant values to implement a priority mux

features.name: register with async reset and constant reset value
features.description: Code should include a register with asynchronous reset where the reset provides an explicit constant initial value

metadata.snippet: always @(posedge clock) q <= reset ? 10'd0 : q;

features.name: register with async reset using constant value
features.description: Code should include a register with asynchronous reset where the reset value is an inlined constant rather than an intermediate wire

features.name: basic interface definition
features.description: Code should include a bare interface definition with signal declarations inside an interface...endinterface block

features.name: interface instantiation inside module
features.description: Code should include an interface type being instantiated as a local variable inside a module body

features.name: interface port on module
features.description: Code should include a module that takes an interface instance as a port parameter

features.name: preprocessor macro with hierarchical path body
features.description: Code should include a `define macro whose replacement body is a hierarchical dot-separated signal reference (e.g., instance.signal), exercising the interaction between the preprocessor and hierarchical name resolution

metadata.snippet: `define ref_Top_inner_x_probe inner.input_probe

features.name: module definition and macro definition sharing a name prefix in the same file
features.description: Code should include both a module definition and a `define macro whose name begins with the module name as a prefix, placed in the same compilation unit, to stress ABI and naming collision detection

features.name: module with probe output lowered to internal wire
features.description: Code should include a module that has a probe-typed output whose lowered SV representation is an internal wire with no corresponding formal output port, reflecting CIRCT's probe-lowering transformation

features.name: clock-typed signal used as register data input
features.description: Code should include an always_ff register whose data (D) input is driven by a clock signal rather than a plain logic/integer data signal, creating a non-integer-typed register scenario

features.name: packed struct register type in sequential logic
features.description: Code should include an always_ff register whose type is a packed struct rather than a plain integer or logic vector, representing a non-integer-typed register

features.name: macro as module name in instantiation
features.description: Code should include a module instantiation where the instance type name is specified via a preprocessor macro rather than a literal module identifier

features.name: define macro for configurable module name selection
features.description: Code should include two or more `define directives that assign different concrete module names to the same macro symbol to select between implementation targets at compile time

features.name: variadic multi-operand AND chain in continuous assignment
features.description: Code should include a continuous assign statement where five or more bit-select operands from a packed input vector are chained together with bitwise AND, forming a variadic AND expression that stress-tests the LowerVariadic synthesis pass

features.name: shared AND sub-expression between two outputs where one is a strict operand subset
features.description: Code should include two continuous assign statements where the AND operands of one output are a strict subset of the AND operands of another output, forming a common sub-expression that a synthesis pass should reuse rather than duplicate

features.name: bit-select from packed input vector in combinational logic
features.description: Code should include combinational logic that individually selects multiple bits from a packed input vector using bit-select expressions and combines them with bitwise operators in continuous assignments

features.name: ltl delay operation without explicit clock
features.description: Code should include an ltl.delay operation that takes input, delay value, and optional length without an explicit clock operand

metadata.snippet: ltl.delay %input, %delay, %length : !ltl.type

features.name: ltl clocked_delay operation with explicit clock and edge
features.description: Code should include an ltl.clocked_delay operation with mandatory clock, edge, input, delay, and optional length operands

metadata.snippet: ltl.clocked_delay %clk, %edge, %input, %delay, %length : !ltl.type

features.name: ltl clock operation for broadcasting clock context
features.description: Code should include an ltl.clock operation that broadcasts clock to a subtree of delays

metadata.snippet: ltl.clock %clk, %edge, %input : !ltl.type

features.name: parameterized interface class declaration
features.description: Code should include a parameterized interface class declaration with a type parameter

metadata.snippet: interface class Bar #(parameter N); endclass

features.name: system task call with variable number of arguments
features.description: Code should include a system task or function call that accepts a variable number of arguments, exercising the ImportVerilog argument-count dispatch path

features.name: system task call with empty argument
features.description: Code should include a system task call that contains an empty or omitted argument, producing an EmptyArgument expression in the AST

features.name: unsupported system task triggering name-based dispatch
features.description: Code should include a less-common or implementation-unsupported system task call so that name-based dispatch can produce a meaningful diagnostic rather than failing on argument conversion

features.name: interface port on module (non-modport interface type)
features.description: Code should include a module that declares an interface-typed port (not a modport), so the port expects a raw interface reference rather than a flattened value

features.name: interface with input direction signals passed to interface port
features.description: Code should include an interface that contains signals used as inputs, and a module that accepts that interface as a port, triggering port flattening where input signals must retain their reference type

features.name: interface instantiation connected to interface-typed module port
features.description: Code should include an interface instance that is connected directly to another module's interface-typed port (not through a modport), where the interface contains at least one input signal

features.name: memory initialization from external file
features.description: Code should include a memory declaration with initialization from an external file using $readmemh or $readmemb

metadata.snippet:
logic [31:0] mem [8192];
initial $readmemh("rom.txt", mem);

features.name: sync read memory with file initialization
features.description: Code should include a synchronous read memory declaration with file-based initialization using $readmemh

features.name: cross_file_macro_definition_and_conditional
features.description: Code should include a macro definition in one file and a conditional directive checking that macro in another file

features.name: macro_conditional_with_undefined_reference
features.description: Code should include an `ifndef directive guarding an undefined macro reference that triggers an error

metadata.snippet:
`ifndef FILE_A
  `err
`endif

features.name: variadic_and_across_multiple_assignments_with_subset_pattern
features.description: Code should include multiple assignment statements where one expression is a strict subset of another, with variadic AND operations on bit-selects from the same vector

features.name: bit_select_vector_for_variadic_reduction
features.description: Code should include bit-selects from a vector used in variadic AND reduction operations across multiple statements

features.name: instance with inner symbol in generate case block
features.description: Code should include an hw.instance with an inner symbol (sym attribute) defined inside a generate case block

features.name: hierpath referencing inner symbol from generate case
features.description: Code should include a hierpath that references an inner symbol defined inside a generate case block, used in XMR operations

features.name: sv_force with xmr to instance in generate case
features.description: Code should include an sv.force operation using an XMR reference to an instance inside a generate case block via a hierpath

features.name: generate case with named begin block and instance
features.description: Code should include a generate case statement wrapped in a named begin block, containing an hw.instance with an inner symbol

features.name: enum built-in traversal method call
features.description: Code should include an enum variable invoking a built-in traversal method like next(), prev(), first(), last(), or num()

features.name: enum first method invocation
features.description: Code should include an enum variable calling the first() built-in method to get the first enum value

metadata.snippet:
typedef enum {FIRST, SECOND, THIRD} e_t;
e_t val = val.first();

features.name: enum last method invocation
features.description: Code should include an enum variable calling the last() built-in method to get the last enum value

metadata.snippet:
typedef enum {X, Y, Z} color_t;
color_t last_val = last_val.last();

features.name: enum num method invocation
features.description: Code should include an enum variable calling the num() built-in method to get the number of enum values

metadata.snippet:
typedef enum {RED, GREEN, BLUE} palette_t;
int count = palette_t.num();

features.name: continuous assignment with rise/fall delay
features.description: Code should include a continuous assignment with rise/fall delay specification using #(rise, fall) syntax

metadata.snippet: assign #(1, 2) o = i;
